channel.title: Stock Market on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Stock Market on MinWeb

item.title: Allbirds Pivots to AI: Stock Soars 300% After GPU Shift
item.description:
If you’ve been feeling lately like you’ve accidentally slipped into a simulation written by a bored screenwriter who has run out of ideas, you aren’t alone. Just when we thought we’d seen the most bizarre corporate pivots in history, Allbirds—the brand famous for making cozy, sustainable wool sneakers—decided to hang up its shoelaces and chase the AI gold rush.

On April 15, 2026, the company rebranded to “NewBird AI.” They’re ditching footwear entirely to become a GPU-as-a-Service (GPUaaS) provider. Honestly, I’m still waiting for someone to tell me this is an elaborate April Fools’ joke that arrived two weeks late.

item.title: Market Dip: Should You Invest or Pay Down Your Mortgage?
item.description:
It’s a feeling every investor knows: you open your brokerage app, see a sea of red, and feel a pit in your stomach. You look at your bank account and wonder, “Should I keep throwing good money into these volatile stocks, or just pay down my mortgage and get a guaranteed return?”

This is the ultimate personal finance dilemma. When the market tanks, the urge to retreat to the safety of home equity is powerful. It feels like “locking in” a win. But is that actually the smart move, or are you just sabotaging your future self? Honestly, most people panic-sell because they’re tired of checking their phones every five minutes.
